title: BBC programme on Elvis Presley's stop at Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: In March 1960, the Rock n' Roll icon set foot on Scottish soil for the first and only time (Image: Prestwick Airport) A NEW programme has shed further light on Elvis Presley's famous stop at Prestwick Airport. In March 1960, the Rock n' Roll icon set foot on Scottish soil for the first and only time on his way back from military service in Germany.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Despite the stopover lasting just 90 minutes, he was swarmed by adoring fans desperate to get a photo with him. Among them was a 16-year-old Anne Murphy, who dashed along to the airport to catch a glimpse of the star. She has now recalled the experience in a new episode of the BBC's Witness History programme.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: More than six decades later, Anne tells Megan Jones what it was like to see Elvis up close. BBC programme on Elvis Presley's stop at Prestwick Airport | Ayr Advertiser
